questions.clientScenario: Fictional company Northbridge Retail says new hires get account access in under 2 hours. As auditor, you need evidence that access provisioning is controlled.
evidenceOptions: Signed access request tickets approved by line managers for the last 3 months
A marketing slide saying onboarding is fast
A list of all employees with no approval trail
IAM workflow screenshots showing approval gates
questions.clientCurveballResponse: The process is performed verbally and no formal records were retained for the sample requested.
questions.followUpQuestion: Given the client response, what is the strongest next audit action?
followUpAnswerOptions: Request a time-bounded, approved source record and define a secure submission channel.
Accept the verbal explanation and proceed without additional corroboration.
Collect all raw sensitive data immediately to avoid delays.
Close the test as effective based on intent rather than evidence quality.

followUpFeedback.0: Strong follow-up. You preserved audit quality, recency, and evidence handling discipline.
followUpFeedback.1: Weak follow-up. Verbal assurances do not satisfy objective evidence requirements.
followUpFeedback.2: Unsafe follow-up. Auditors should minimize collection of sensitive data and request sanitized extracts.
followUpFeedback.3: Weak follow-up. Control effectiveness cannot be concluded without sufficient appropriate evidence.
